what_it_does: An interpretive JSON ledger record that describes and classifies a SYNTHESIS-PLAN.md document, summarizing its purpose, structure, notable claims, conventions, open questions, themes, novelty, and intended contribution to a fused design-system build.
observations: Identifies the analyzed document as a bridge plan that maps confirmed design-analysis findings to concrete changes in the ConceptV design system.
Orders work by readiness and dependency: foundation tokens before container structure before template library.
Section 1 proposes token recalibrations with explicit deprecate-don't-rename and keep-the-color-mix-machinery guidance.
Section 2 lists new tokens for hatch, blueprint ghost, frame signature, bullets, status traffic-light, and 3:2 plus web frame ratios.
Section 3 centers on a recursive typed-container library (Band/Section/Zone/Panel/Cluster/atoms) with role, spacing rhythm, collapse rule, and depth-keyed wash.
Sections 4-7 cover templates, axis controls, motion/space-time, and the deck-to-app bridge, each gated on prior structural decisions.
Suggests a first execution slice: token recalibration, refresh zone specimen, then pause for the container-library decision.
CAPSTONE reduces the whole system to one type system, one rule engine, two layout solvers (block flow-stacking and graph relational), axis-dials, and an invariant DNA core.
Uses a readiness legend: green ready now, yellow ready after a structural decision, blue confirm with more folders.
Cross-references sibling analysis docs as the source of confirmed findings.
standouts: Capstone reduction: one type system + one rule engine + two layout solvers (block + graph) under axis-dials over an invariant DNA core.
One container model deliberately serving both presentation decks and the live app as a single mutable tree.
Explicit dependency ordering: foundation values -> structure containers -> library templates.
conventions: Readiness-emoji legend tied to evidence and risk.
Each planned section names a concrete WHERE (file or directory) for the change.
Deprecate-don't-rename / keep-old-as-legacy guidance to avoid breaking consumers.
Cross-references sibling analysis documents as the source of confirmed findings.
concerns: Leaves key reconciliation questions open rather than resolving them inside this record.
notes: A metadata interpretation of a build-transition plan that treats the recursive container library as the keystone decision and the token recalibrations as the safe first slice.
questions: Are the specific near-white hex targets here reconciled with the slightly different values quoted in REQUIREMENTS.md / recent-pitches.md?
Which product-UI-heavy ingest folder is intended to de-risk the deck-to-app bridge in section 7?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the record is titled and functions as a readiness-ordered, dependency-aware mapping from findings to concrete build steps.
apparent_themes: One generative core serving every surface (deck and app)
Parameters and tokens over one-off layouts
Deliberate structural decisions gated rather than rushed
Single-source token recalibration with backward-compatible deprecation
intention_signals: Trying to sequence the move from design analysis into a real build so the riskiest piece (the recursive container library) is a deliberate, well-supported decision, while the low-risk fidelity gains (tokens) land first.
novelty: The explicit reduction of the entire design system to one type system + one rule engine + two layout solvers (block and graph) under axis-dials, and using the same container tree for both decks and the app.
contribution: Contributes the readiness/dependency-ordered build sequence, the token-recalibration target values, the recursive container architecture, and the capstone unified-core formulation that a fused system would keep as its build map.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive ledger record describing a readiness- and dependency-ordered synthesis plan that maps confirmed design findings to concrete builds, culminating in a unified generative core.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the transition plan from design analysis to real build, emphasizing foundation-first sequencing and the container library as a deliberate keystone decision.
